messages.author: IdeaDigital - Martin
messages.text: I see plenty of messages from North Heath & from Shotley near Ipswich— but they don’t hear my replies. The messages via North Heath come from all over England 14 or 15 hops. Some even come from Rainham or Epping, but have to go south of the river to get to me. Messaging works locally though with the MK repeater in Colchester. I’ve been rotating the repeater today to try and work out if it is showing a bias in one direction. Be interested to hear if you saw any weak signals at a specific time?

messages.author: IdeaDigital - Martin
messages.text: Interesting lift on last night and this morning -- managed to pick up BE and NL neighbours on the co6-rep01 repeater. I also managed to log in as a guest to North Heath Omni and check their stats and Neighbours. Bad news I'm afraid Bob. They are completely deaf to us in Essex The repeater also shows 0 RX packet errors -- which I can only assume is because it cannot hear weak signals? I might consider filtering/blocking North Heath Omni as a one-way repeater is a distraction for other mesh users who end up wasting loads of time trying to respond with messages that will never make it back to the sender.

messages.text: Hi chaps. Just thought I’d introduce myself. I’m a Uk ham, I’ve tried to build some free tools that you can use to help establish or strengthen your mesh. The site is called Meshrank.net. I’m actually looking for someone who would like to act as an observer in this area. If you setup an Observer node you also get access to some private functions like a Live map. Hope it’s of interest to You. It’s very much work in progress. MeshRank.net

messages.author: IdeaDigital - Martin
messages.text: Always worth setting up a portable repeater and scouting the locations first. Looks like there's a natural route between Colchester and Erith -- we've christened it Diagon Alley -- which is where we are getting all of our data from the rest of the UK. It's one-way only though. And doesn't work at all when Colchester and Galleywood lower the repeaters for windy weather like we've got right now Because Colchester data is fed into MeshRank.net, you can see if you are being heard by checking their Message feed. I think that we all have Guest access open on the repeaters too, so log in and take a look when you are in range.

messages.text: Hi Bob, that cofirms that I'm not getting anything at all from Galleywood. I purged the companion last night and rebooted co6. A few days ago I was getting about a third of the packets direct from North Heath and the rest from Galleywood + D130 to Galleywood and on to co6. That has completely dried up and you and D130 no longer appear on the analyser.letsmesh.net map. We've also lost our link to Ipswich since the Shotley repeater got damaged in the wind -- so it's really obvious that North Heath is our only source of packets from outside of Colchester. There were 25 repeaters in Ipswich last week, plus Galleywood, D130 and a couple of other South Essex spots on the map. Now they've all gone (edited)

messages.text: Ipswich has a network of around 30 repeaters at the moment, so messages and adverts should be heard from nearly everywhere. Ipswich run their own web site which publishes information about the repeaters and nodes and includes adverts and Public messages. Here's a link to the Messages section: https://ipnt.uk/messages You'll see that they picked up my messages as I headed to the Naze Tower this morning -- from 10:26 onwards. The messages would have been received by the Walton repeater and then relayed into the Ipswich network via Felixstowe. So, if you head north towards Walton, you will be able to Message and then check the IPNet web site to see if you're making it into Ipswich. Alternatively, if you head towards Colchester and get within range of either of the co6 repeaters, your Messages will appear here: https://meshrank.net As both repeaters are sending MQTT data to MeshRank (and letsmesh too). Currently Colchester isn't linking with Ipswich. We were linked via Shotley, but the repeater got damaged in the winds last week. Hopefully it'll be repaired this weekend. Also the CO4 repeater will also link Colchester to Ipswich, but needs a filter... so it's deaf right now. There was somebody else in Clacton recently who was on the Facebook UK Meshcore Group asking about getting connected. I'll see if I can find their details?

messages.author: Richard Clark
messages.text: Lardon my ignorance but what do the dB readings on each repeater mean ?
messages.content_html: Lardon my ignorance but what do the dB readings on each repeater mean ?
messages.reply_html:
IdeaDigital - Martin
Weird. I’ve had Tuk as a neighbour for 48 hours — going through Galleywood, but not getting you

messages.author: IdeaDigital - Martin
messages.text: Signal strength. + dB is strong, - dB is weak. So it's a good indicator of link quality scale. +5 to +10 dB → Excellent (very reliable, plenty of margin) 0 to +5 dB → Good (stable) -5 to 0 dB → Usable but marginal -10 to -5 dB → Weak (packet loss likely) Below -10 dB → Very weak (barely decoding / intermittent) (edited)
messages.content_html: Signal strength. + dB is strong, - dB is weak. So it's a good indicator of link quality scale. +5 to +10 dB → Excellent (very reliable, plenty of margin) 0 to +5 dB → Good (stable) -5 to 0 dB → Usable but marginal -10 to -5 dB → Weak (packet loss likely) Below -10 dB → Very weak (barely decoding / intermittent) (edited)
messages.reply_html:
Richard Clark
Lardon my ignorance but what do the dB readings on each repeater mean ?

messages.author: Richard Clark
messages.text: Thanks, so it’s broadcast strength ?
messages.content_html: Thanks, so it’s broadcast strength ?
messages.reply_html:
IdeaDigital - Martin
Signal strength. + dB is strong, - dB is weak. So it's a good indicator of link quality scale. +5 to +10 dB → Excellent (very reliable, plenty of margin) 0 to +5 dB → Good (stable) -5 to 0 dB → Usable but marginal -10 to -5 dB → Weak (packet loss likely) Below -10 dB → Very weak (barely decoding / intermittent) (edited)

messages.author: IdeaDigital - Martin
messages.text: No, it's more like SNR-like behaviour, not power -- which is measured in dBm. So negative values still work as LoRa allows you to decode messages that are lost in the noise. Is confusing as both use dB, but the numbers in the picture are relative measurements of signal quality. Whereas a dBm value for a transmitter/receiver is a measureable absolute power level -- so would always be + (edited)
messages.content_html: No, it's more like SNR-like behaviour, not power -- which is measured in dBm. So negative values still work as LoRa allows you to decode messages that are lost in the noise. Is confusing as both use dB, but the numbers in the picture are relative measurements of signal quality. Whereas a dBm value for a transmitter/receiver is a measureable absolute power level -- so would always be + (edited)

messages.author: IdeaDigital - Martin
messages.text: Tested another repeater on the same mast and the results are the same: a couple of messages and contact per hour, if I'm lucky. Basically, it means that I get a fraction of the data from North Heath Omni, filtered by D130, 45 (which doesn't seem to advertise, so no name) and Bob's Galleywood repeater. So, fed up with the silence, I've put CO6 back up and normal service has been resumed -- hundreds of messages and contacts -- direct from North Heath Omni. I don't know why it works, but it works. Shame it's one-way, but I'd rather see what's going on on the mesh than sit about waiting for somebody local to send a message.

messages.author: IdeaDigital - Martin
messages.text: You can easily feed mqtt data to letsmesh temporarily. Go to their web site and download the Observer script. This runs from any computer on the command line using python. You connect companion via Bluetooth and it automatically feeds mqtt data to letsmesh. I have it running on a laptop and set to launch only when I choose. So, as long as I have a web connection and a companion with me, I can set up anywhere and feed adverts and messages into letsmesh. You can also adapt the script to feed meshrank at the same time.
